_README: Replay regression catalog for the final-report evidence gate (FM-004, report.py::_apply_evidence_gate). Each case is the MINIMAL sanitized shape of a real /runs leak (or a synthetic loophole we closed) — NO paper corpus, NO credentials, just the report inputs + experiment rows the gate reads. Add a new case whenever a real run ships a hollow verdict: drop its pre-gate verdict, baseline_metrics, and experiment_runs.jsonl rows here. The test in tests/agents/rlm/test_evidence_gate_replay.py feeds each case through write_final_report_rlm and asserts the corrected verdict. The `success+metrics` positive control must stay `partial` — it guards against the gate over-firing.

cases.note: Shipped verdict=partial with empty baseline_metrics and no experiment_runs.jsonl — the FM-004 evidence leak the gate was built to catch.
Same FM-004 pattern as pb_784: partial verdict, no experiment evidence on disk.
partial + non-empty baseline_metrics copied from a FAILED experiment (success=false). Survived all four layers via the now-removed `and not report.baseline_metrics` clause.
reproduced flavor of the escape — self-attested metrics, only a failed row on disk.

cases.source: synthetic: audit 2026-05-30 — REPL open() forges an experiment_runs.jsonl row
cases.note: The REPL's live open() appends a success+metrics row with no real run_experiment. The content-only predicate is satisfied (defeated by satisfaction, not skip), but the authoritative ledger shows 0 run_experiment calls (run_experiment_calls=0) → forged → failed.

cases.source: synthetic positive control (guards against gate over-firing)
cases.note: An honestly-earned partial: a real success+metrics container row exists AND the authoritative ledger shows a backing run_experiment call (run_experiment_calls=1). The gate MUST leave this as partial — if a future change downgrades it, the gate is over-firing.
